Options.OPTION_DESCRIPTION[RWH.Realistic: Load Asset Pack Settings
Disable workplace calculations for airports.
Disable workplace calculations for depots and cargo.
Disable workplace calculations for fire stations.
Disable workplace calculations for transportation stations.
Disable workplace calculations for admin buildings.
Disable workplace calculations for garbage facilities.
Disable workplace calculations for hospitals.
Disable workplace calculations for police stations.
Disable workplace calculations for parks.
Disable workplace calculations for post offices.
Disable workplace calculations for power plants.
Disable workplace calculations for schools.
Percentage of apartments or houses that will be free and reserved for the local population of the city. (Default: 5)
Average Floor Height for commercial, office, and city services buildings. (Default: 3.05)
If selected, gas stations will have fewer workers (Default: not selected)
If true, low density houses will only have one household (Default: selected)
Average Floor Height for residential buildings. (Default: 3)
Average Apartment Size in Square Meters. (Default: 90)
Average Apartment Size in Square Meters. (Default: 150)
Number of Apartments per Elevator. The Elevator area will be subtracted and reduce the space available for apartments in the building. (Default: 70)
Options.OPTION[RWH.RealisticWorkplacesAn: Percentage of floor space for hallways
Options.OPTION_DESCRIPTION[RWH.Realistic: Percentage of floor space for hallways. Does not include space for elevators and stairs. (Default: 10)
Options.OPTION[RWH.RealisticWorkplacesAn: Disable Apt. per floors for Row Homes
Options.OPTION_DESCRIPTION[RWH.Realistic: If disabled, Row Homes will use the average sqm per apartment defined above. Otherwise, a specific number of apartments per floors will be used for Row Homes. (Default: not selected)
Number of apartments per floor in Row Homes. (Default: 1)
Options.OPTION[RWH.RealisticWorkplacesAn: Disable Fewer Households On Luxury High Rises
Options.OPTION_DESCRIPTION[RWH.Realistic: Number of apartments will be reduced once the building reaches levels 4 and 5. WARNING: This feature may cause performance issues and more homeless people in bigger cities. (Default: not selected)
Increase in apartment size when compared to the average apartment size. (Default: 10)
Increase in apartment size when compared to the average apartment size. (Default: 20)
If set to true, Row Homes will have an extra floor in the basement. (Default: selected)
Average Floor Height for industry buildings. (Default: 4.5)
For industry buildings that have a larger area than the defined threshold, a reduction factor will be applied to reduce the number of workplaces calculated. (Default: 7000)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 50)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 50)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 95)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 65)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 120)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 45)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 65)
Options.OPTION[RWH.RealisticWorkplacesAn: Recreation and Entertainment: Square Meters per Worker
Options.OPTION_DESCRIPTION[RWH.Realistic: Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 93)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 37)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 50)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 80)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 200)

Increases the amount of electricity produced based on the increase of employees compared to the vanilla game. If there is a decrease of employees, power production will not change. (Default: not selected)
Reduce the calculated worker count for Solar Power Plants by the specified factor. (Default: 5)
Hospital: Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 100)
Clinic: Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 110)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 40)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 25)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 55)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 46)
Hospital: Number of square meters per patient. Higher numbers will decrease the hospital capacity. (Default: 50)
Clinic: Number of square meters per patient. Higher numbers will decrease the clinic capacity. (Default: 35)

The total amount of space required to have one elevator. This area will be used to calculate the number of elevators in the building, and the elevator area will reduce the available space for workers. (Default: 4180)
Number of square meters per worker. Higher numbers will decrease the number of workers. (Default: 23)
Percentage of area in Office Buildings that are non-usable. This includes for example: hallways, conference rooms and mail rooms. It does not include space for elevators and stairs. (Default: 20)
For buildings that have higher number of floors as defined with this threshold a reduction factor will be applied to reduce the number of workplaces calculated. (Default: 60)
Number of Students per Teacher. This will be used to calculate the number of workers required at schools. (Default: 14)
Percentage of staff at schools that are not teachers. (Default: 30)
Space required for each student. This will be used to calculate the school capacity. (Default: 7)
This factor will be applied to the base square meters per student for Colleges. (Default: 3)
This factor will be applied to the base square meters per student for Universities. (Default: 4)
Reduce the cost of service upkeep. Use this to compensate for the extra cost due to more workers at service buildings. (Default: 70)

Reduce electricity consumptions. Use this to compensate for the increase of electricity consumption in buildings. Electricity consumption is directly related to number of residents or employees in a building, so this mod will cause buildings to use more electricity. (Default: 20)

Reduce water consumptions. Use this to compensate for the increase of water consumption in buildings. Water consumption is directly related to number of residents or employees in a building, so this mod will cause buildings to use more water. (Default: 20)
Scales noise produced by buildings. 100% = vanilla noise. 0% = no noise. Example: 25% means buildings produce 25% of the vanilla noise (75% reduction).
This mod may cause higher rents due to changes in the number of building households. Use this setting to set a rent discount that will compensate the increase in rent. (Default: 20)
Reduce all workplace and househols results by this percentage. Note that some buildings need to have a minimum of 1 worker or household and those might not be reduced. (Default: 0)
Number of square meters per prisoner. Higher numbers will decrease the number of prisoners. (Default: 4)
Options.OPTION[RWH.RealisticWorkplacesAn: Number of Prisoners per Correctional Officer
Options.OPTION_DESCRIPTION[RWH.Realistic: Number of Prisoners per Correctional Officer. This will be used to calculate the number of workers required at prisons. (Default: 4)
Exports CSV files containing detailed data (height, lot size, population, etc.) for all building prefabs to your Documents folder.
Reset settings to default values
Percentage of area in Prison Buildings that are as living quarters for prisoners. This include hallways, cafeterias, medical facilities, offices, etc. (Default: 40)
Select what action to do with evicted households. They can either look for a new home or be deleted. (Default: ResetType.FindNewHome)
Disable calculations for households. Household values for residential buildings will be set to vanilla. Requires restarting the game. (Default: not selected)
Disable calculations for workplaces on Office, Commercial, and Industrial zones. Workplace values will be set to vanilla. Requires restarting the game. (Default: not selected)
Disable calculations for workplaces on City Services buildings. Workplace values will be set to vanilla. Requires restarting the game. (Default: not selected)
This factor affects the speed of houshold spawning. Lower values means more households. Vanilla value is 0.5 (Default: 0.5)
Multiplier for households of low-density asset pack buildings. 1.00 = default, values less than 1 reduce, values more than 1 increase.
Multiplier for households of row homes asset pack buildings. 1.00 = default, values less than 1 reduce, values more than 1 increase.
Multiplier for households of medium and high-density UK asset pack buildings. 1.00 = default, values less than 1 reduce, values more than 1 increase.
Change asset pack factors settings (Default: PacksEnum.France)
Save the current sliders for the selected pack.
If selected, power plant worker counts will be calculated using Employees per GW (option below). If not selected, workers are calculated using Square Meters per Worker.
Number of workers per GW of power plant capacity/production. Only used if 'Use Employees per MW' is selected.
